scope: Codex-native compaction boundaries, token/cache economics, mathematical context policy, evaluation research, and closest prior art

verdictDefinitions.VERIFIED: The cited primary or official source directly supports the claim. Author-reported benchmark numbers remain author-reported unless independently reproduced.
verdictDefinitions.INFERENCE: A product or engineering conclusion derived from verified facts; it still requires ContextGC-specific testing.
verdictDefinitions.UNKNOWN: The inspected public primary sources do not establish the claim.
verdictDefinitions.REJECTED: Primary evidence or established prior art contradicts the proposed claim.

claims.claim: Codex hooks expose SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, and Stop lifecycle events suitable for a context control plane.
claims.counterevidence: Event presence does not imply every desired action is available at that event.
claims.productDecision: Use hooks for observation, bounded injection, snapshots, audit, and fail-closed control only.

claims.claim: SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit can add model-visible developer context.
claims.counterevidence: Injected developer context is not the same as replacing or editing native conversation history.
claims.productDecision: Inject only the latest schema-valid bounded Task Frame and preserve a strict token cap.

claims.claim: PreCompact can stop compaction with continue=false, while PostCompact runs only after compaction and cannot undo it.
claims.counterevidence: Neither event documents an action for inspecting the opaque compacted state.
claims.productDecision: Snapshot before compaction and measure the event afterward; do not promise semantic inspection or rollback of native compaction.

claims.claim: Current Codex hook execution supports command handlers; prompt and agent handlers are parsed but skipped, and async command hooks are unsupported.
claims.counterevidence: Future Codex releases may broaden handler support.
claims.productDecision: Implement synchronous command hooks and gate compatibility by tested Codex versions.

claims.claim: The transcript_path hook field points to a transcript whose format is not a stable hook interface.
claims.counterevidence: A format can remain unchanged for several releases without becoming a supported contract.
claims.productDecision: Use a versioned tolerant adapter, preserve unknown records, and fail closed on unsupported schemas.

claims.claim: A normal plugin hook can directly start native compaction in an arbitrary existing Codex Desktop or CLI thread.
claims.counterevidence: The documented PreCompact surface can veto but has no start-compaction action; documented thresholds are configuration values.
claims.productDecision: Do not ship or advertise plugin-driven native actuation in P0.

claims.claim: A client that owns a Codex app-server thread can request thread/compact/start and observe contextCompaction lifecycle items.
claims.counterevidence: This does not grant control over a thread owned by another client, and parts of app-server transport are experimental.
claims.productDecision: Keep app-server actuation as an opt-in, version-pinned experimental adapter.

claims.claim: Codex exposes static auto-compaction threshold and scope configuration.
claims.counterevidence: A config value does not establish safe dynamic mutation during a live thread.
claims.productDecision: Read configuration for simulation context; do not rewrite user configuration automatically.

claims.claim: OpenAI Responses compaction returns an opaque encrypted compaction item that carries forward state using fewer tokens.
claims.counterevidence: Opaque continuation does not prove retention of every exact fact or downstream task quality.
claims.productDecision: Evaluate compaction behavior through hidden task tests and exact invariant probes, not content inspection.

claims.claim: OpenAI prompt-cache hits depend on exact prefix matches, and API responses report cached-token and GPT-5.6-family cache-write categories.
claims.counterevidence: API prompt-caching accounting does not prove identical accounting for ChatGPT-authenticated Codex sessions.
claims.productDecision: Track cache categories when reported and label all parity assumptions explicitly.

claims.claim: Codex usage is determined by prompt length alone.
claims.counterevidence: OpenAI states that model, context, reasoning, tool use, retrieval, caching, task size, and task duration all affect usage.
claims.productDecision: Report disaggregated usage signals and never infer efficiency from prompt length alone.

claims.claim: There is a public deterministic conversion from local ChatGPT-authenticated Codex token events to actual credits charged per run.

sources.title: Work mode admin FAQ - usage and cost

claims.counterevidence: The inspected official docs describe variable usage and warn against exact workflow attribution from aggregate analytics; no conversion formula is given.
claims.productDecision: Set estimatedCredits to null for ChatGPT-login runs; expose weightedTokenProxy and optional estimatedApiEquivalentUsd.

claims.claim: The periodic cost model C(L)=K/L+pg(L-1)/2 has a continuous stationary optimum L*=sqrt(2K/(pg)).

sources.title: A First Order Approximation to the Optimum Checkpoint Interval

claims.counterevidence: The ContextGC equation is an engineering proxy, not Young's failure model and not a theorem about semantic quality. Nonstationary tasks can have no useful interior optimum.
claims.productDecision: Use it only as a baseline; add lifecycle boundaries, hysteresis, cooldown, and a safety cap.

claims.claim: Checkpoint placement is an optimization trade-off among save cost, restore/rework cost, task duration, and failure behavior.

sources.title: On the Optimum Checkpoint Selection Problem

claims.counterevidence: The source's explicit stochastic failure models do not match unknown semantic loss in coding agents.
claims.productDecision: Borrow the cost/recovery framing but fit no failure distribution in P0.

claims.claim: Model Predictive Control depends on an explicit model and repeated finite-horizon optimization.

sources.title: Model predictive control: Theory and practice - A survey

claims.counterevidence: The source does not say MPC is appropriate for LLM context management.
claims.productDecision: Defer MPC until transition and horizon forecasts are calibrated and beat the deterministic controller.

claims.claim: A small additive KEEP/SUMMARIZE/EXTERNALIZE selection model with integer budgets can be solved exactly by discrete dynamic programming.
claims.counterevidence: Exactness disappears if semantic utility is mismeasured or non-additive interactions are introduced.
claims.productDecision: Limit the exact claim to the declared finite optimization instance and expose inputs in the receipt.

claims.claim: Classical submodular approximation guarantees apply to any ContextGC utility score without further proof.

sources.title: Best Algorithms for Approximating the Maximum of a Submodular Set Function

claims.counterevidence: The guarantee requires a defined nondecreasing submodular objective and the stated constraint class; ContextGC has not established those properties.
claims.productDecision: Use deterministic heuristics or exact DP; advertise no generic submodular guarantee.

claims.claim: Prompt compression has a task-dependent rate-distortion trade-off, and query-aware compression was important in the NeurIPS 2024 study.

sources.title: Fundamental Limits of Prompt Compression: A Rate-Distortion Framework for Black-Box Language Models

claims.counterevidence: Its distortion-rate optimum is defined for the paper's model/distribution and evaluated largely on synthetic and small natural-language data.
claims.productDecision: Define distortion through hidden task tests and exact invariants, not text similarity alone.

claims.claim: RECOMP showed that end-task-trained retrieval compression and selective augmentation can greatly shorten retrieved context on its language-modeling and QA tasks.

sources.title: RECOMP: Improving Retrieval-Augmented LMs with Compression and Selective Augmentation

claims.counterevidence: The evaluated tasks do not prove safe preservation of exact coding constraints or tool outcomes.
claims.productDecision: Require downstream coding-task success and forbid semantic compression of exact protected values.

claims.claim: LongMemEval evaluates information extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge updates, and abstention over long interactive histories.

sources.title: LongMemEval: Benchmarking Chat Assistants on Long-Term Interactive Memory

claims.counterevidence: It is conversational QA rather than a deterministic software-engineering benchmark.
claims.productDecision: Borrow update, temporal, and abstention dimensions while using coding fixtures with hidden graders.

claims.claim: Conformal Risk Control can control expected bounded monotone losses under its calibration assumptions.
claims.counterevidence: A semantic coding-loss proxy may be non-monotone, and exchangeability between calibration and future coding trajectories is unproven.
claims.productDecision: Make no conformal guarantee in P0; first collect independent labeled trajectories and validate the loss family.

claims.claim: Learn then Test provides a finite-sample parameter-calibration framework through multiple hypothesis testing.

sources.title: Learn then Test: Calibrating Predictive Algorithms to Achieve Risk Control

claims.counterevidence: Three handcrafted fixtures do not provide a credible independent calibration and test population.
claims.productDecision: Freeze thresholds before confirmatory local replay, but call this preregistration rather than statistical certification.

claims.claim: Magic Context already implements cache-deferred reductions, tagged raw history, bounded expansion, background summarization, and structured facts.
claims.counterevidence: Repository documentation is maintained by the project and its performance claims are not independent evaluation.
claims.productDecision: Reject novelty based on cache awareness, raw expansion, tiering, or background history compression alone.

claims.claim: LCM proposes a hierarchical summary DAG with pointers to original material for targeted retrieval.
claims.counterevidence: The label lossless concerns raw retrievability; the active summary can still omit semantic detail, and benchmark claims are author-reported.
claims.productDecision: Describe ContextGC archives as byte-recoverable, never its summaries as lossless.

claims.claim: Hermes Agent already supports threshold compression, a pluggable context engine, and native Codex app-server compaction modes.

sources.title: Hermes Agent context compression and caching

claims.counterevidence: Hermes owns a different agent/runtime surface from an installable pure Codex plugin.
claims.productDecision: Differentiate through native plugin lifecycle receipts and invariant gates, not auto-compaction itself.

claims.claim: GitHub Copilot CLI automatically compacts long sessions and explicitly documents that fine-grained details may be lost.

sources.title: Managing context in GitHub Copilot CLI

claims.counterevidence: The documented thresholds and behavior apply to Copilot CLI, not Codex.
claims.productDecision: Make loss risk visible and demonstrate exact-source recovery rather than implying summaries preserve everything.

claims.claim: Claude Code supports automatic and manual compaction and documents which instruction surfaces survive or may be lost.
claims.counterevidence: Claude Code behavior does not establish Codex behavior.
claims.productDecision: Treat durable invariant reinjection as established practice rather than novelty.

claims.claim: Letta already provides always-visible structured memory blocks and separate archival memory/search.

sources.title: Letta resources for agents - archival memory

claims.counterevidence: Letta is a stateful agent platform, not a Codex-native plugin.
claims.productDecision: Do not claim first typed or tiered agent memory.

claims.claim: LangGraph already supports durable checkpoints, replay, fork/time travel, and short/long-term memory stores.
claims.counterevidence: LangGraph checkpoints apply to graphs built on that runtime, not arbitrary Codex threads.
claims.productDecision: Differentiate on Codex integration and auditability, not checkpoint/replay as a general concept.

claims.claim: TokenPilot studies cache-efficient, lifecycle-aware context management for long-running agents.

sources.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents

claims.counterevidence: Reported savings are author-reported and benchmark-specific.
claims.productDecision: Reject novelty based only on cache-aware timing or lifecycle eviction.

claims.claim: ACON reports optimizing compression guidelines from full-context-success versus compressed-context-failure trajectories and reducing peak tokens on its tasks.

sources.title: ACON: Optimizing Context Compression for Long-horizon LLM Agents

claims.counterevidence: The paper reports compressor overhead, limited model coverage, and benchmark-specific results; the numbers are not ContextGC evidence.
claims.productDecision: Use paired failures as research inspiration, but ship a deterministic controller and local receipts.

claims.claim: SWE-MeM trains coding agents to choose when, what, and how to compress long trajectories.

sources.title: SWE-MeM: Learning Adaptive Memory Management for Long-Horizon Coding Agents

claims.counterevidence: Results are author-reported and require training; they do not validate an untrained Codex plugin controller.
claims.productDecision: Reject novelty based only on adaptive memory actions for coding agents.

claims.claim: MAGE (Memory as Agent-Guided Exploration) proposes hierarchical execution-state trees with grow, compress, maintain, and revise operations.

sources.title: Beyond Semantic Organization: Memory as Execution State Management for Long-Horizon Agents

claims.counterevidence: The MAGE acronym is also used by unrelated papers; the cited meaning must be spelled out, and results are author-reported.
claims.productDecision: Describe Task Frame lineage precisely and avoid acronym-only comparisons.

claims.claim: ContextGC is the world's first automatic, cache-aware, reversible context manager for coding agents.
claims.counterevidence: Each major component of that claim has explicit prior art.
claims.productDecision: Claim only the Codex-specific combination of invariant gates, provenance, receipts, and reversible archives.

claims.claim: ContextGC compression is lossless and never forgets.
claims.counterevidence: Summaries can omit fine detail and native compaction is opaque. Only separately archived bytes can be proven recoverable.
claims.productDecision: Use 'byte-recoverable archive' and report retention tests; never use lossless for active summaries.

claims.claim: Usage Proxy per Verified Success is an established externally validated measure of Codex credits.
claims.counterevidence: UPVS is a ContextGC experiment metric with frozen weights; it is not a credit receipt or a published OpenAI billing metric.
claims.productDecision: Label UPVS as a comparative proxy and always show raw token categories.

residualUnknowns.question: Will OpenAI expose authoritative per-run credits for ChatGPT-authenticated Codex?
residualUnknowns.impact: Blocks exact-credit optimization and savings claims.

residualUnknowns.question: Which transcript record shapes remain compatible across the intended Codex release range?
residualUnknowns.impact: Requires compatibility fixtures and fail-closed adapters.

residualUnknowns.question: Can a supported plugin API initiate native compaction in an existing host-owned thread in a future Codex release?
residualUnknowns.impact: Determines whether P0 advisory behavior can later become native actuation.

residualUnknowns.question: Do atom utility and risk scores predict hidden coding-task success on independent tasks?
Does cache accounting in ChatGPT-authenticated Codex follow the public API prompt-caching schedule closely enough for economic decisions?
residualUnknowns.impact: Blocks transfer of API cache-cost calculations to local subscription usage.

residualUnknowns.question: Is there enough independent trajectory data to justify conformal or Learn-then-Test calibration?
residualUnknowns.impact: P0 must use deterministic safety gates instead of statistical guarantees.

claimPolicy.allowedTemplate: ContextGC is a Codex-specific, invariant-gated and reversible context controller. In frozen local traces it changed a declared weighted token proxy while preserving every critical fixture invariant.
